stem: A manufacturing quality inspection uses 3 parallel inspectors for redundancy. The defect caught requires at least one inspector to function. inspector 1 has a reliability of 78%. inspector 2 has a reliability between 71% and 89% (uncertain due to limited operational data). inspector 3 has a reliability of 76%. What is the overall probability that the defect caught?

ground_truth.answer: P(defect caught) ranges from 0.9847 to 0.9942. Point estimate: 0.9894.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 inspectors fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.22 × 0.29 × 0.24 = 0.0153. P(works) = 0.9847.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.22 × 0.11 × 0.24 = 0.0058. P(works) = 0.9942.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
